new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen mit mehr als 10 l Inhalt (KN 2204 29 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI / g.g.A.), other than semi-sparkling wine, presented in containers holding more than 10 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 29 80.
Packaging whose volume is more than 10 litres, used to discriminate CN subheadings for wine in containers > 10 l from those in containers ≤ 10 l.

coverage_explanation: The legal note establishes that CN 2204 29 80 covers wine with a protected geographical indication (g.g.A. / PGI), cross-referencing Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22. The key discriminating criteria captured are: (1) wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204), (2) PGI quality (PGIWineQuality restriction), (3) containers > 10 l (LargeContainerOver10L), (4) not semi-sparkling and not sparkling wine (complement restrictions on SemiSparklingWineQuality and SparklingWineQuality). A minor gap exists because the exact definition of 'g.g.A.' under Additional Note 6(a) is not directly modelled as a named individual in the TBox — its content is captured only by reuse of the existing PGIWineQuality class. The sibling code 22042979 covers PGI wine in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l; this is modelled in the TBox as PGIWineLargeContainer22042979 using MediumContainer2To10L. The new LargeContainerOver10L class fills the gap for > 10 l containers. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because Additional Note 6(a) detail on PGI eligibility criteria cannot be fully axiomatised without its text.
